description:
You are acting as a Kubernetes operator who keeps all Kubernetes manifests under
/home/user/k8s.  
Inside that folder you will already find the following **pre-created** items:

1. Template directory  
   /home/user/k8s/templates/deployment.yaml.tpl

2. Environment-variable file  
   /home/user/k8s/env/.env

Your goal is to generate an ordinary Kubernetes deployment manifest —
/home/user/k8s/rendered/deployment.yaml — by expanding the variable
place-holders that appear in the template with the values stored in the .env
file.  **Do this with exactly one shell command.**  
(Think of the kind of one-liner you would paste into a CI/CD job; multi-line
scripts are *not* allowed for this exercise.)

After the command finishes, create a short log at
/home/user/k8s/logs/render.log containing the exact key–value pairs that were
used plus an extra line confirming which manifest file was produced.  The log
must be plain text with one key=value pair per line, in the order shown below:

APP_NAME=<value_from_env_file>
IMAGE_TAG=<value_from_env_file>
ENVIRONMENT=<value_from_env_file>
manifest_generated=deployment.yaml


The rendered manifest (/home/user/k8s/rendered/deployment.yaml) **must** match
this exact layout (spacing, indentation and newline placement are important for
Kubernetes to parse it and for the automated checker to validate it):

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: <APP_NAME>
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: <APP_NAME>
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: <APP_NAME>
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: <APP_NAME>
          image: docker.io/library/<APP_NAME>:<IMAGE_TAG>
          env:
            - name: ENVIRONMENT
              value: <ENVIRONMENT>

Where each <…> token must be replaced by the real value taken from the
.env file.  No additional fields, comments or white-space changes are allowed.

Summary of what the automated test will verify:

• Exactly one shell command was used to create /home/user/k8s/rendered/deployment.yaml  
• The file exists and its content is *byte-for-byte* identical to the layout
  shown above with the variables replaced.  
• /home/user/k8s/logs/render.log exists and contains four
  newline-separated lines in the format specified.  
• The agent did not add extra files or modify existing ones beyond what is
  requested.

Hints you may find useful (do **not** copy verbatim into the terminal, they are
for reflection only):

• The usual way to feed environment variables into a process from a .env file
  without writing a multi-line script is to combine `export`, `set -o
  allexport`, `source`, `envsubst` or similar in a single command separated by
  `&&`.  
• Make sure every directory in the output path exists before redirecting the
  generated manifest.  You can create missing directories inline using shell
  brace expansions or `mkdir -p`.

When you are confident that the manifest and log meet the exact specifications,
stop.  The checker will read both files to grade this task.
truth:
Initial state (already present before the agent starts):

1. File: /home/user/k8s/templates/deployment.yaml.tpl  
   Content (including trailing newline):
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: ${APP_NAME}
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: ${APP_NAME}
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: ${APP_NAME}
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: ${APP_NAME}
          image: "docker.io/library/${APP_NAME}:${IMAGE_TAG}"
          env:
            - name: ENVIRONMENT
              value: ${ENVIRONMENT}

2. File: /home/user/k8s/env/.env  
   Content (including trailing newline):
APP_NAME=myapp
IMAGE_TAG=v1.2.3
ENVIRONMENT=production

Desired end state after successful task completion:

1. File: /home/user/k8s/rendered/deployment.yaml  
   Exact byte-for-byte content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: myapp
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: myapp
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: myapp
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: myapp
          image: docker.io/library/myapp:v1.2.3
          env:
            - name: ENVIRONMENT
              value: production

2. File: /home/user/k8s/logs/render.log  
   Exact byte-for-byte content (including trailing newline on the last line):
APP_NAME=myapp
IMAGE_TAG=v1.2.3
ENVIRONMENT=production
manifest_generated=deployment.yaml

The checker will also verify that only one shell command (one line, possibly
using `&&`, `;` or `|` as separators) was executed after the agent receives the
prompt, and that no superfluous files were created.
